question: Explain the role of the CVCS (Chemical and Volume Control System).

nuscale_ch01_0366.title: DSRS 9.3.6, Rev 0: Containment Evacuation and Flooding SystemsII.1 GDC 2
nuscale_ch01_0366.summary: The section summarizes NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for containment evacuation and flooding systems, specifically noting compliance with GDC 2 under DSRS 9.3.6, Rev 0. It states that the NuScale design meets the requirements with no exceptions or additional comments for this criterion.

nuscale_ch01_0361.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.5 Chemical and Volume Control System Design and Arrangement
nuscale_ch01_0361.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS), including the Boron Recovery System. It states that the NuScale CVCS generally conforms to the applicable DSRS requirements, with partial conformance noted only where specific criteria do not apply to the NuScale design, such as holdup tanks subject to vacuum conditions. The section also clarifies that certain SRP criteria are not applicable to NuScale’s PWR design.

nuscale_ch01_0363.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.7 Prevention of CVCS Holdup Tank Wall Buckling/Failure; CVCS Venting and Draining
nuscale_ch01_0363.summary: The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report states that the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) partially conforms to DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0, Section II.7 regarding prevention of holdup tank wall buckling/failure and venting/draining. The partial conformance is due to differences in the NuScale design, which does not include holdup tanks subject to vacuum conditions, but the design does provide appropriate venting and draining capabilities as required.

nuscale_ch01_0364.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.8 ITAAC
nuscale_ch01_0364.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS), including the Boron Recovery System, as outlined in DSRS 9.3.4. The NuScale design generally conforms to the criteria, with partial conformance noted only for requirements related to holdup tank buckling/failure, as the specific tank design referenced does not apply. All other criteria, including ITAAC, are met or not applicable.

nuscale_ch01_0359.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.3 Minimization of contamination
nuscale_ch01_0359.summary: This section summarizes NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS), including the Boron Recovery System, focusing on contamination minimization. It notes partial or full compliance with various safety and design criteria, highlighting that the primary CVCS safety function is to prevent inadvertent boron dilution, and that single-failure criteria apply specifically to safety-related isolation valves. Some criteria are not applicable due to differences in design or previous regulatory approvals.

nuscale_ch01_0357.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.1 CVCS Functional Performance during Adverse Environmental Phenomena; Pumping Capacity; and defense-in-depth RCS makeup
nuscale_ch01_0357.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) and related systems. It notes that the CVCS partially conforms to requirements for functional performance during adverse environmental conditions, with its only safety-related function being the prevention of inadvertent boron dilution in the reactor coolant system. Additionally, the system fully conforms to single failure criteria and other design requirements for safety-related isolation valves.

nuscale_ch01_0047.title: Annex Building
nuscale_ch01_0047.summary: The Annex Building (ANB) is a nonsafety-related structure in the NuScale Power Plant that serves multiple support functions. It controls access to radiologically and non-radiologically controlled areas, provides personnel support services (such as locker rooms, showers, and first aid), houses decontamination equipment and dosimeter processing, and supports plant security with facilities like a secondary alarm station and security offices.

nuscale_ch01_0045.title: Other Site Structures
nuscale_ch01_0045.summary: This section describes the functions and features of other site structures at a NuScale Power Plant, including the Turbine Generator Buildings (TGBs), Annex Building (ANB), and Security Buildings (SCBs). The TGBs house turbine generator sets and maintenance facilities, the ANB provides personnel support and security functions, and the SCBs manage site access control. All these structures are classified as nonsafety-related.

nuscale_ch01_0358.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.2 Single Failure Criteria and GDC 5
nuscale_ch01_0358.summary: The section states that the NuScale Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) conforms to the single failure criteria and General Design Criterion (GDC) 5, specifically for the two safety-related demineralized water isolation valves that prevent inadvertent boron dilution of the reactor coolant. The single-failure criteria are only applicable to these valves, ensuring safety in this critical function.

Section B provides a functional description of the CVCS's operational role in startup and boron control, which supports the regulatory conformance and contamination minimization requirements outlined in Section A.

Section B describes specific operational roles and functions of the CVCS, providing concrete examples that illustrate and support the general regulatory performance expectations outlined in Section A.
